user_prompt: Write a cover letter for Notion that mentions their writing culture and references my docs work at my last job.
actual_output:
### Cover Letter — Notion

I'll need a couple of details to make this sing, but here's a strong draft you can edit. Replace the bracketed parts:

---

Dear Notion Hiring Team,

I've been a Notion user since [year] — long enough to remember when databases were the new shiny thing — and what's kept me on the platform isn't the feature set. It's the writing. Notion is one of the few software companies where the docs feel like they were written by someone who actually wanted you to understand, not just legally cover the product. Ivan's essays, the changelog voice, even the empty-state microcopy — there's a craft there that's rare.

That writing culture is a big part of why I'm applying for the [Role Title] position.

At [Last Company], I [owned / led / overhauled] our internal documentation for [system/product/team]. When I started, [brief problem — e.g., "engineering onboarding took three weeks and most of it was tribal knowledge passed by Slack"]. I rewrote [X docs / the entire onboarding wiki / our API reference] with a focus on [clarity, examples-first structure, voice — pick what's true]. The result: [concrete outcome — e.g., "onboarding dropped to 8 days," "support tickets on [topic] fell 40%," "the docs became the #1 referenced resource in our team Slack"].

Beyond the metrics, I genuinely like writing docs. I think of them as a product — with users, edge cases, and a maintenance burden. That's the same lens I'd bring to [specific Notion team or product area — e.g., "the Help Center," "AI feature documentation," "developer-facing API docs"].

I'd love to talk about how I can contribute to [specific Notion initiative or value — e.g., "the work your team is doing on AI-assisted writing," "Notion's evolving docs as you ship Calendar and Mail"].

Thanks for reading,
[Your Name]

---

### To finalize, send me:
1. **Exact role title** (Technical Writer? Content Designer? Docs Engineer?)
2. **Your last company + the docs project** — what was broken, what you did, what changed
3. **One Notion product area** you'd want to work on (Help Center, AI, API docs, marketing site, etc.)

Once I have those, I'll deliver the final version and queue up the application. Want me to also check Notion's current open roles to confirm which posting this should target?
judge_reasoning: The chatbot directly fulfilled the user's request by providing a tailored cover letter draft that incorporated the specific themes requested (Notion's writing culture and previous documentation work).
